text:
The CSS looks correct. The `.tab-pane { display: none; }` should hide all tab panes by default, and `.tab-pane.active { display: grid; }` should show only the active one.

But wait - I see the issue! The `.dashboard-grid` class has `display: grid` on line 195, which is applied to ALL elements with that class. When I have:

```html
<div class="dashboard-grid tab-pane active" id="tab-dashboard">
```

This element has BOTH classes: `dashboard-grid` AND `tab-pane` AND `active`.

The CSS rule `.dashboard-grid { display: grid; }` applies to this element, setting `display: grid`. The CSS rule `.tab-pane { display: none; }` should override this because `.tab-pane` has the same specificity as `.dashboard-grid` (both are single class selectors) and comes LATER in the CSS file.

But wait, I need to think about this more carefully. The rule `.tab-pane { display: none; }` comes AFTER `.dashboard-grid { display: grid; }`, so it should override it. Then `.tab-pane.active { display: grid; }` should override `.tab-pane { display: none; }` when the element has the `active` class.

So the CSS should work correctly. Let me verify the JavaScript is actually working by adding some console logs and checking if the `active` class is being properly added and removed.

Actually, I think the issue might be simpler. The user might be seeing the page BEFORE the JavaScript fix I made. The JavaScript file had a syntax error (`autoSkip: trueLimit: 6,`) which prevented it from loading. I fixed that syntax error, but maybe the frontend server is serving a cached version of the JavaScript file.

Let me verify that the frontend is serving the correct (fixed) JavaScript file.
 
I'm sensing a potential misunderstanding about the tab layout. The user wants completely separate views for each tab, not stacked content. This suggests the current implementation might be showing all tab contents simultaneously instead of hiding inactive tabs. I'll need to investigate the HTML structure and CSS to ensure only the active tab is visible.

The key is to confirm the CSS rules for hiding and showing tabs are working as expected. I'll carefully examine how the `.tab-pane` and `.active` classes are being applied to make sure each tab's content is properly hidden or displayed.
